title: calliandra yoroensis
intro: Calliandra yoroensis, also known as Pink Powderpuff, is a flowering plant native to Central America, characterized by its vibrant pink flowers and feathery foliage. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Calliandra yoroensis include wound healing, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory effects, making it a valuable resource for natural remedies. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat skin conditions, fever, and respiratory issues.
